column1: #mapping_set_description: "Cross-ontology mappings between the Personal Agent Ontology and the Semantically-Interlinked Online Communities (SIOC) ontology for conversation threading interoperability."

column8: Both represent coherent dialogue sequences. PAO Conversation is a BFO process; SIOC Thread is an information container.
PAO Turn is a temporal process (one participant contribution); SIOC Post is an information item. Strong functional equivalence.
Both represent communication content. PAO Message is the content of a Turn; SIOC Post combines the act and content.
PAO Session is a bounded interaction period (process); SIOC Container is a generic grouping construct.
Both represent roles played by participants. PAO AgentRole is BFO-aligned (prov:Role); SIOC Role is simpler.
PAO Agent is broader (includes AI agents); SIOC UserAccount is a human user identity.
PAO HumanUser is a person; SIOC UserAccount is an account identity. Close but ontologically distinct (person vs account).
Both link content to topics/subjects via SKOS concepts.
Both represent the textual body of a communication item.
PAO partOfConversation links turns to conversations; SIOC has_container links posts to threads.
PAO CommunicationChannel is a medium for agent interaction; SIOC Site hosts online communities. Both are interaction venues, but PAO is medium-focused while SIOC is platform-focused.
